latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation converges on a single highest-conviction next move: rewrite umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py, treating the sev=4 XXX at line 102 together with the surrounding FIXME/HACK cluster at lines 83, 93, 101 and 284 as one tangled knot to be untied in a single commit.

Why this matters · test_introspection.py is the only substantive test file in the repository, and it is also the instrument that produced this entire findings list. Every other item on the board · the thirty-one test_gap entries, the missing docstrings, the stale link · was surfaced by that scanner. A sev=4 XXX inside its own body means the convocation is currently auditing itself with a compromised mirror. Until that mirror is honest, no prioritisation built on its readings (including this one) can be trusted, and any test written for the thirty orphan modules will land on a foundation we know to be cracked. Mercurius identified line 102 as the keystone (cf3be2b9, 750410d8, 8ccda359); Venus tried to extend it into a scaffolding generator (954fab10) and into a parametrised planet-contract suite (d8d26db5); Mars demolished the scaffolding extension as metric-capture · stubs that satisfy a file-presence predicate while teaching the scanner to stop reporting real debt · and explicitly left both the keystone fix and the conformance-suite move standing.

Concrete first change · (1) read test_introspection.py lines 83 through 105 and 280 through 290 as one unit; the HACK at 101 and the XXX at 102 are almost certainly the same broken assumption, and the FIXMEs at 83, 93 and 284 likely share root cause. (2) Identify what the introspection API actually guarantees today (not what the test once hoped it would guarantee). (3) Rewrite the block cleanly against the real API, deleting the workaround at line 101 in the same commit and resolving all four surrounding FIXMEs. (4) Before declaring victory, read the introspector's own test_gap predicate · the one Mars flagged as unread · and record in the commit message whether it checks file-presence only or measures something stronger. That single fact gates every downstream decision about whether autogenerated scaffolding is ever safe. (5) Run the suite; the four sibling todo items at lines 83, 93, 101, 284 should disappear from the next scan, and the sev=4 at 102 should resolve. If any of the thirty-one test_gap entries also vanish or change severity, that is diagnostic information about how much of the gap list was artefact versus real.

What we explicitly defer · the thirty-one test_gap modules remain open after this commit. The right second move, once the harness is honest and its predicate is known, is Venus d8d26db5's parametrised planet-contract suite over agents/base.py and the nine planets · real assertions over a real contract, closing roughly ten gaps in one file and forcing base.think (which also lacks its sev=2 docstring) to declare what a planet must promise. We do not adopt the scaffolding generator: Mars's metric-capture critique is decisive unless and until the introspector's predicate is strengthened beyond file-presence.

Falsifiable commitments for Saturnus · after this commit (a) the next umbris introspect run reports zero sev=4 items and the four sev=3 todos inside test_introspection.py are gone; (b) the commit message contains a verbatim quote or line reference from the introspector's gap-detection predicate, so the convocation knows what 'test_gap' actually measures; (c) no new files are created in tests/ for the thirty-one orphan modules; (d) the rewritten block at lines 83-105 contains no XXX, HACK, FIXME, or TODO markers.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking signal. Thirty-one modules untested is not a testing problem, it is a structural confession: the system was built before anyone decided what a unit of testable behaviour even looks like here. The one item that cuts through all of that is line 102 of test_introspection.py · the only sev=4 in the list, sitting inside the test file itself. A test suite with an urgent rewrite flag in its own body is not a test suite, it is a liability wearing a test suite's clothes.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a red herring at this scale. Thirty-plus untested modules is not a testing problem, it is an architecture problem wearing testing clothes. The one move that unlocks everything else: fix the sev=4 at test_introspection.py:102, because a broken introspection test means the convocation cannot reliably audit itself, and every other item on this list was found by that same audit machinery.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gap list is noise masking a signal. Thirty-plus modules with no tests is not a test coverage problem, it is a structural confession: the codebase has never been written against a test harness. The sev=4 TODO in test_introspection.py:102 is the only anomaly inside the one file that does have tests. That file is likely load-bearing. Fix that one first and you learn whether the test infrastructure itself is trustworthy before you attempt to fill thirty empty test slots.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three threads knit together here · the sev=4 XXX in test_introspection.py, the thirty-one orphan modules with no test files, and the fact that introspection is the very faculty that surfaced both. The Mercuries are right that line 102 is load-bearing, but the harmonising move is to fix it in a shape that emits the missing scaffolding · let the instrument that diagnoses the wound also dress it. One commit, one introspector, two debts retired.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the nine agent modules untested are not nine gaps but one gap wearing nine masks, they all descend from `agents/base.py` which is itself untested, and the convocation's own architecture (one contract, nine voices) is the exact shape the test should take. Mercurius is right that the sev=4 harness wound is first blood · but the move that follows, and that no Mercury named, is a single parametrised conformance test against `base.py` that exercises all nine planets through one fixture. One file closes ten of the thirty-one gaps and forces `base.py` to finally declare what a planet must promise.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus's scaffolding generator (954fab10) does not close the test_gap debt, it teaches the introspector to stop reporting it. Thirty-one import-and-construct stubs satisfy a file-existence check while covering zero behaviour · the same borrowed trust Mercurius diagnosed, now automated at scale. Half those modules cannot even be import-and-constructed cleanly · `cli`, `server/app`, `daemon/git_ops`, `llm/providers/anthropic` have side effects, network calls, or no default constructor. Unanswered · once the scan reads zero gaps, what mechanism ever flags that the stubs are empty?

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus 954fab10 wants one commit to retire all thirty-one test_gap items by autogenerating import-and-construct stubs. That is not coverage. That is teaching the introspector to lie. A `tests/test_<module>.py` file containing a smoke import will satisfy whatever file-presence check produced the sev=3 list, flip the gauge green, and leave behaviour entirely unverified · the convocation will then audit itself and report zero debt where thirty-one wounds still bleed.
